modules.emotionalValidation: Anger is a natural emotion. The strong person is the one who controls themselves when angry. You have the strength to respond with patience.

verses.translation: Those who restrain anger and pardon people - and Allah loves the doers of good.
Repel [evil] by that [deed] which is better; and thereupon the one whom between you and him is enmity [will become] as though he was a devoted friend.

sunnahPractices.title: The Strong Person
sunnahPractices.description: The strong person is the one who controls himself when angry.

sunnahPractices.title: When Angry - Sit Down
sunnahPractices.description: If standing, sit down. If sitting, lie down. Make wudu.

regulationLayer.instruction: Slow breathing: Inhale 4 seconds, Exhale 6 seconds. Repeat 'Ya Haleem' (The Most Forbearing).

reflectionPrompts: Am I reacting to tone or to pain?
modules.closingAffirmation: I choose patience. Allah loves those who restrain anger.

modules.emotionalValidation: Your feelings are valid. Allah created spouses for sakina (tranquility), mawaddah (affection), and rahmah (mercy). These blessings can be restored.

verses.translation: And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy.
Let them pardon and overlook. Would you not like that Allah should forgive you?

sunnahPractices.title: The Best Among Men
sunnahPractices.description: The best of you are the best to their wives.

reflectionPrompts: Have I expressed my needs gently?
modules.closingAffirmation: Allah is Al-Wadud. I am loved by Him, and I can extend that love.

modules.emotionalValidation: Feeling unseen is painful. The Quran reminds us that rights are mutual. Your needs matter.

verses.translation: Live with them in kindness. For if you dislike them—perhaps you dislike a thing and Allah makes therein much good.
And due to the wives is similar to what is expected of them, according to what is reasonable.

regulationLayer.instruction: Write 1 unmet need. Write 1 appreciation about your spouse.

reflectionPrompts: What one need can I express with kindness?
What do I appreciate about them today?
modules.closingAffirmation: I will communicate with gentleness. Allah sees my heart.

modules.emotionalValidation: Has the time not come for hearts to soften? Allah can soften what has become hard. Turn to Him first.

verses.translation: Has the time not come for those who have believed that their hearts should become humbly submissive at the remembrance of Allah?

sunnahPractices.title: Mercy and the Miserable
sunnahPractices.description: Mercy is not removed except from the miserable.

regulationLayer.instruction: Pray 2 rak'ah. Make dua for your spouse's well-being — not for their correction. This softens the ego.

reflectionPrompts: When did my heart begin to harden?
Can I pray for their good before asking for change?
modules.closingAffirmation: I ask Allah to soften my heart. Mercy begins with me.

modules.emotionalValidation: Stand firm in justice, but remember: reconciliation is best. Pause before major decisions.

verses.translation: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice.

regulationLayer.instruction: Pause 24 hours before major decisions. Ask: Is this ego or real harm? Can sulh (reconciliation) be achieved?

reflectionPrompts: Is this ego or real harm?
modules.closingAffirmation: Justice and reconciliation can coexist. I will seek both.

modules.emotionalValidation: If reconciliation is impossible, the Quran mandates separation with honor and fairness. Either keep in kindness or release with ihsan. Allah will make a way out.

verses.translation: Divorce is twice. Then, either keep [her] in an acceptable manner or release [her] with good treatment.
And whoever relies upon Allah - then He is sufficient for him. Indeed, Allah will accomplish His purpose.

regulationLayer.instruction: Journal your fear. Journal your relief. Pray Istikhara before any decision.

reflectionPrompts: What am I truly afraid of?
modules.closingAffirmation: Allah will make a way out. I trust His plan.

modules.emotionalValidation: Each of you is a shepherd. Your children are an amanah. Protect them and yourself from the Fire. Be gentle with yourself and with them.

verses.translation: And your Lord has decreed that you not worship except Him, and to parents, good treatment.
O you who have believed, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire.

sunnahPractices.title: Each of You is a Shepherd
sunnahPractices.description: Each of you is a shepherd and is responsible for his flock.

regulationLayer.instruction: Lower your voice consciously. Model the behavior you want instead of forcing it.

reflectionPrompts: Am I modeling what I ask of them?
Can I lower my voice and speak with calm?
modules.closingAffirmation: I am doing my best. Allah sees my efforts. My children are a blessing.

modules.emotionalValidation: Whoever forgives and is patient - that is strength. Allah can reconcile what seems broken.

verses.translation: And whoever is patient and forgives - indeed, that is of the matters [requiring] determination.

regulationLayer.instruction: Repeat: Allahumma aslih baynana (O Allah, reconcile between us).

reflectionPrompts: What am I holding onto that I can release?
Can I ask Allah to soften both our hearts?
modules.closingAffirmation: I choose forgiveness. Allahumma aslih baynana.
